guide.subtitle: Let's get your congregation set up in 4 easy steps

step1.title: Create Options
step1.what: Create labels used to classify households. Each option has a code (shown on the map), a description (shown in menus), and three settings: Default, Countable, and Sequence.
step1.why: Default sets the pre-selected type when a new household is added. Countable options count toward the territory completion percentage. Sequence controls the order options appear in publisher menus.

step2.title: Create Territory
step2.what: Create a named geographic zone with a unique code and name. Each territory acts as a container that holds multiple maps (buildings or addresses).
step2.why: Territories divide your congregation's area into manageable sections. Publishers are assigned maps within a territory, making it easy to coordinate who covers which area.
step2.example: Examples: Downtown, North District, Block A1-A5, Zone 3

step3.title: Select Territory
step3.where: Select Territory dropdown menu at the top
step3.what: Pick which territory you want to add maps to
step3.why: Maps must belong to a territory. Selecting a territory first tells the system where to place your new maps.

step4.title: Create Maps
step4.where: Create Map button (after selecting a territory)
step4.what: Add a specific building or address to your selected territory. A map can be single-storey (a row of units) or multi-storey (floors with individual units).
step4.why: A territory holds all its maps together. Publishers work through each map marking individual households. Once created, use the Assign or Personal buttons on each map card to share access with publishers.
step4.example: Examples: 123 Main St, Apartment Complex B, Shopping Mall, 456 Oak Avenue

update.title: Update Available

unsupported.title: Unsupported Access Method 📱
unsupported.message: This app does not support being opened as an installed app (PWA). Please open it directly in your web browser instead.
